verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (BiP/GRP78) is an ER-luminal chaperone canonically resident in the ER lumen. However, it has documented surface-accessible pools in two key contexts: (1) cell-state-induced ecto-presentation in cancer cells — tumor cells display HSPA5 on the outer leaflet as an immunogenic stress-antigen, recognized by antibodies and CAR-T programs; (2) cell-death-induced surface exposure — apoptotic and necroptotic cells externalize HSPA5 to the plasma membrane. These non-baseline surface displays qualify as contextual / cell_state_induced.
